STATE UNIVERSITY OF NEW YORK RK STATE COLLEGE FOR TEACHERS

coLiace oF epucarion ar ABI nea Frank G. Carrino ™) 3-1254 &x.73

OFFICE OF THE PRESIDENT

September 1, Albany, N.Y. - The College of Hiueation at Albany awarded certificates to 28 high school teachers who satisfactorily eonpleted the August driver training-safty courses, unier the direction of Professor | ‘thomas R. Gibson, Chairman ef the Department of Safty and Health Eduostion at the College. d

4n qvard luncheon at Forborts marked the completion of the tvo courses which ran concurrently: a three-week basic course in the Teaching of Driver Education, end a two-week course on the Problema in the Administration ani Teaching of Driver ani Traffic Sefaby Education. ‘Those courses, according te Professor Gibson, are designed “to prepare instructes to institute and teach driver education ani teach driver education courses in their schools, or to give inoteuetors efvanced proparation in order to improve their teaching abilities." Many of these instructors, representing high schools through- out Mew York, wore enabled to attend these courses through scholarship grants nade available by the Allistate Foundation. These funds were the eighth enmual grent by this Tountation.

fmong those present at tbe luncheon were: John &. Arens, Director of Public Relations of the Allstate Insurance Company, White Plains, N.¥.3 Biward Way, of Marsh Hallman Chevrolet, Inc. Ernest W. Manning, President of the Albany Auto Club, Inc., ani Drs, Edgar W. Flinton, Willaim E, Seymour, ani Frank @, Carrino of the College administrative staff.

Professor Gibson, along with Associate Professor Harry L. Clawson of

(nore)
